title: Read Signs & Wayfinding
summary: Understand airport, hospital, and building signs to find your way.
learnerPromise: Understand airport, hospital, and building signs to find your way.
whyItMatters: Airports, hospitals, malls, and highways have their own sign systems. Understanding them keeps you calm, saves time, and helps prevent wrong turns in high-stakes places.
realLifeUses: Find your gate at a busy airport during a tight layover.
Navigate a hospital to reach the right department or ward.
Read highway exit signs to know when to get off.
Find restrooms, exits, or elevators in a large shopping mall.
youWillLearn: Recognize common sign types: directional, informational, regulatory.
Follow color-coded and symbol-based wayfinding systems.
Read airport and hospital signage accurately under pressure.
Identify exit routes and safety signs in a building.
miniChallenge: Look up an airport terminal map online and trace a path from check-in to a gate.
steps: When you enter a building, pause and look for the main directory or map.
Identify the color or symbol system — each may represent a different wing or floor.
Follow the signs toward your destination. Confirm each turn with the next sign.
If signs conflict or stop, return to the last main junction and look again.
Note the exit signs as you go so you know how to get back.
completionCriteria: You can navigate an unfamiliar large building using its internal signage.
You can identify exit routes and safety signage in a public space.
commonProblems: Signs point in conflicting directions — look for the most recent or most specific sign.
You missed a turn because you were rushing — slow down, signs are there to help.
The building has multiple wings or floors — confirm the zone or level.
tips: Directories near entrances usually show 'you are here' — start there.
International symbols are consistent — learn the ones for exit, restroom, and elevator.
If you are lost in an airport, follow the crowd — most people are heading toward gates or baggage claim.
